adjunction.recognition: NONE. Rmk 5.3: for an arbitrary free-forgetful adjunction the left adjoint need not be fully faithful, so the counit does not recognize free objects -- a free module is not usually recovered from the free module on its entire underlying set. Freeness is the essential image (Cor 9.2), recognized by the inhabitedness of Pres_{F_R}(M), not by the counit.

arrows.provenance: Gram constructor (Foundations Def 21.3): G |-> (R^n, its standard basis, b_G(x,y) = x^T G y). Half of a named EQUIVALENCE with the inverse G_ij = b(e_i,e_j); after restricting to SymMat_n(R)^nd it lands in coordinatized lattices. It constructs coordinatized forms in general and is not the ontology of the named root lattices (Rmk 21.4).
the locus where u_rho is an isomorphism (Def 68.5)

discard the root basis; the Grothendieck projection of Def 68.5. A named ADE lattice is the image here of a chosen point upstairs (Construction 68.6), and Thm 68.7 makes that image independent of the standard realization chosen.

adjunction.localization_class: the J-local equivalences, f with a_J(f) an equivalence (Def 8.2); sheafification exhibits Sh as the localization of PSh at that class
adjunction.recognition: REFLECTIVE (Conv 14.3): the inclusion is the fully faithful RIGHT adjoint, so the UNIT recognizes -- a presheaf is a sheaf iff its unit is an equivalence (Prop 5.2, Def 6.1). Dual to the monoid-algebra case, which is coreflective and recognized by its counit; Conv 14.3 forbids one unqualified phrase for both.
adjunction.unit: F -> i_J a_J F, the localization morphism

essential_image_factorization.note: Cor 8.3: a colimit of sheaves is the reflector applied to the presheaf colimit -- composition with the reflector, not a separate colimit construction (Prop 6.4).
essential_image_factorization.objects: the J-local presheaves, i.e. the sheaves

arrows.provenance: sheafification a_J, a LEFT EXACT left adjoint to the inclusion (add-II Thm 8.1)

adjunction.recognition: REFLECTIVE, as for sheafification (Conv 14.3). Rmk 9.2: these are named instances of reflective localization, and must not be represented by unrelated axioms or by a bare containment claim. Stacks in groupoids come from the appropriate truncation.
adjunction.unit: P -> its stackification; a prestack is already a stack precisely when this is an equivalence

arrows.provenance: stackification a_J^st (add-II Thm 9.1); the same reflective-localization construction as sheafification applied to space-valued presheaves

adjunction.role: right adjoint of the chosen lift; see fun.free_operadic_algebra

arrows.provenance: U_O, the operadic forgetful functor (add-II Def 11.1)

adjunction.recognition: NONE claimed. Rmk 5.3 applies as to any free-forgetful adjunction: the left adjoint need not be fully faithful, so the counit does not recognize free algebras. A chosen free presentation of A is a point of Pres_{Free_O}(A) (Thm 11.2).

essential_image_factorization.not_identified_with_source: Rmk 11.3: monadicity of U_O identifies Alg_O(V) with algebras for the monad U_O Free_O; it does not identify all O-algebras with the essential image of Free_O.
essential_image_factorization.note: Rmk 11.4: tensor-power and coinvariants formulae for the underlying object are comparison or computation theorems under stated colimit hypotheses, never the definition of the free-algebra functor.

arrows.provenance: Free_O, left adjoint to the operadic forgetful functor (add-II Thm 11.2)
ev_F : Adj(K) -> Arr(K), evaluation on the generating left adjoint (add-II Def 1.2)
ev_U : Adj(K) -> Arr(K), evaluation on the generating right adjoint (add-II Def 1.2)
the Arr(D^~) leg of the Def 3.1 comma pullback; p_F is evaluation at the target, and its fibres are the presentation spaces
the target of the inclusion, L' (Def 27.1); the ambient L is the source of the arrow
a moduli problem is a prestack (Def 35.1)

classifiers.name: Finite
classifiers.notes: Character (property vs structure) computed from the leg, not stored

classifiers.name: Graded
classifiers.notes: Chosen grading data; forgetful generally not full

classifiers.name: Domain
classifiers.notes: Domain ≠ IntegralDomain; need not be commutative

classifiers.name: WithBasis
classifiers.notes: Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

classifiers.name: Limits
classifiers.notes: Foundations Rmk 14.4: existence of limits is property-like. CHOSEN limits are a different, structure-like classifier with nontrivial fibres; if a consumer needs a choice it must say so.

classifiers.name: Free
classifiers.notes: Freeness is the essential image of the free R-module functor F_R (addendum Def 9.1, Cor 9.2), equivalently the propositional truncation of the existence of a chosen basis (Rmk 13.7) -- hence a property classifier, not structure. The finite restriction F_R|FinSet has the finite free modules as its image.

classifiers.name: Monoidal
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.2: MonCat -> Cat. The fibre over a fixed category can contain SEVERAL inequivalent monoidal structures, so a consumer must name the chosen lift -- (x)_R or (+) on R-Mod are different lifts of the same category. Unnamed structure-reference is the standing lift-ambiguity hazard.

classifiers.name: Nondegenerate
classifiers.notes: AMBIGUOUS BY ITSELF (Rmk 18.4). Retained only for rows that have not yet been assigned a precise condition; Lat_R now uses GenericallyNondegenerate and DiscBil uses Perfect. Any new use must name which of trivial radical, injective adjoint, perfect pairing, or generic nondegeneracy it means.

classifiers.name: WithBasis
classifiers.notes: refined from named-join path | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

classifiers.name: WithBasis
classifiers.notes: authored sagecats ledger | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

classifiers.name: Commutation
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 85.3: on an object of BiAct(R,S) the two composites R (x) M (x) S -> M, r(x)m(x)s |-> (rm)s and r(x)m(x)s |-> r(ms), agree. A PROPERTY classifier -- the full replete locus is _R Bimod_S. So the two actions are structure and their commutation is an additional diagrammatic property, not part of the data.

classifiers.name: Preadditive
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.1: a preadditive category is enriched in abelian groups, and the forgetful map from categories with a CHOSEN preadditive structure is a structure classifier.

classifiers.name: Additive
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.1: finite biproducts, a limit condition stated RELATIVE to a chosen preadditive structure.

classifiers.name: Abelian
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 14.1: kernels and cokernels with every mono and epi normal. 'Being abelian is a property' is always understood relative to the additive enrichment that makes the axioms meaningful.

classifiers.name: RadicalFree
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 18.3: rad(M,b) = ker(b~) = 0. Also called separating. Weaker than perfect.

classifiers.name: Perfect
classifiers.notes: Foundations Def 18.3: b~ is an ISOMORPHISM, equivalently rad(M,b) = 0 AND Def(M,b) = coker(b~) = 0. For an integral lattice perfectness is also called unimodularity. Strictly stronger than radical-free, and conflating the two deletes the defect module that carries discriminant theory (Def 18.2).

classifiers.name: GenericallyNondegenerate
classifiers.notes: Foundations Rmk 18.4, the precise condition named for the arithmetic lattice category: nondegenerate after extending scalars to the fraction field. This is Def 19.2 condition 3, and Prop 25.2 calls perfectness of a discriminant pairing its finite-length torsion COUNTERPART -- counterparts, not the same condition.

definition.notes: Algebras over a field; same OverField classifier as VectorSpaces, on Algebras(R)

entities.provenance: reverse-map vertex; MagmaticAlgebras correspondence via field base

definition.notes: R-module with bilinear multiplication; no assoc/unit required

Foundations Def 85.2: an abelian group with a left R-action and a right S-action, plus the comparison identifying the two underlying additive groups. No commutation is imposed. Both legs are underlying-additive-group functors, so this is a functor-functor pullback, not the functor-against-classifier shape used elsewhere.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 85.2; the two actions are structure and their coexistence is this pullback

Foundations Def 13.8 / 85.1. Derived, not primitive: it is the commutation locus inside BiAct(R,S) (Def 85.2, Def 85.3) -- two actions are structure, their commutation a property. Bimodules(R,R) is this family at equal parameters, not a separate node.

definition.notes: the bimodule family on the diagonal; forgetting either action to Modules(R) is neither an equivalence nor full (Foundations Prop 92.1)

entities.provenance: Foundations Prop 92.1; destination of Sage's Modules(R)

definition.notes: not VectorSpaces(K): by Prop 85.4 this is (K tensor_ZZ K)-Mod, which is K only for a prime field

entities.provenance: the bimodule diagonal over a field; destination of Sage's VectorSpaces
bedrock terminal of the preferred parent spine

Foundations Def 25.3: finitely generated torsion modules with a symmetric PERFECT pairing into K/R. Prop 25.2: perfectness here is the finite-length torsion counterpart of generic nondegeneracy for a lattice, not the same condition, so it must not share a classifier with Lat_R.

definition.notes: DivisionRings := Rings.Division (nonzero multiplicative invertibility). Not Magmas.Inverse.

entities.provenance: dot_import | Foundations Def 58.5 records 'semifan' as explicitly NOT a primitive of the report: no single standard definition was fixed, and any later use must state which face, intersection, local-finiteness and group-invariance axioms are imposed. Its absence from the seed is therefore a documented decision, not a gap.
essential image of the free R-module functor F_R (addendum Def 9.1, Cor 9.2); freeness is a property, a basis is structure (Rmk 13.7)

definition.notes: exceptional common-data host for reflection/Coxeter package

definition.morphisms: A : G -> H with A^T H A = G
definition.notes: Foundations Def 21.2. A skeletal COORDINATE PRESENTATION of the symmetric forms on the standard free module R^n -- explicitly not a second kind of mathematical object. Restricting to matrices nondegenerate over K gives SymMat_n(R)^nd.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 21.2; presentation, compared to the intrinsic forms by the Def 21.3 equivalence

definition.notes: Foundations Def 21.3: coordinatized bilinear forms. Morphisms are abstract form-preserving linear maps; a matrix is obtained from a morphism by applying the chosen source and target bases (Cor 74.5), not by being the morphism.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 21.3; the codomain of the Gram constructor

definition.notes: Distinct presentation from Domains; relation to ax.IntegralDomain not settled in seed

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 19.2: a finite projective R-module with a symmetric form whose scalar extension to the fraction field is nondegenerate. Rmk 18.4 names that precise condition generically nondegenerate; the bare word covers four inequivalent conditions.

definition.notes: LeftModules(R) is the explicit spelling of normalized Modules(R) = R-Mod (Foundations Def 13.1). Sage's LeftModules is the one-sided category; Sage's Modules is not.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.1: R-Mod is primitive (left modules); Cor 90.2 identifies it with (R,Z)-bimodules, no choice involved. Not Sage's Modules(R), which is Bimodules(R,R).

definition.notes: Foundations Def 13.5: objects (M,s) with s : R^n -> M surjective; morphisms preserve the chosen frame. A chosen generating family, not a property.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.5; chosen finite generating presentation as structure

definition.notes: Foundations Def 13.5: objects (M,s) with s : R^n -> M an isomorphism, morphisms STRICTLY frame-preserving (fs = t). Rmk 9.3 case 2, which makes this category equivalent to Set.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.5; a chosen basis with basis-preserving morphisms

definition.notes: Foundations Def 13.6: the same objects as BasisFrame_n(R) but arbitrary R-linear maps. The morphism is the abstract linear map; its matrix relative to the two chosen bases is a derived representative (Cor 74.5). Rmk 9.3 case 1, and the convention Sage's ModulesWithBasis uses.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 13.6; the category for matrix calculus and change of coordinates

definition.notes: WithBasis is the basis-frame comma category over R-Mod (Def 13.5), so on a bimodule it is the pullback of BasisFrame along the left projection U_L

entities.provenance: named composite | Morphisms are arbitrary R-linear maps (addendum Rmk 9.3 case 1): the chosen basis is structure on objects and imposes no condition on maps. 'Modules with basis' never determines its morphisms, and the two conventions give different categories -- case 2, basis-preserving maps, is equivalent to Set. Sage is case 1, checked: in CombinatorialFreeModule(QQ,['a','b']) the map a |-> 2*W['x'], b |-> 0 sends basis vectors to non-basis vectors and is a morphism of the category.

definition.notes: Not an identity with left modules; Mod-R ≃ R^op-Mod under the opposite-ring substitution

definition.fiber: the groupoid of S-indexed root frames in L
definition.notes: Foundations Def 68.5: objects (L, rho); a morphism (L,rho) -> (L',rho') is an isometry f with f rho = rho'.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 68.5; a chosen root frame is structure over the intrinsic lattice

definition.condition: u_rho : Z^(S) -> L is an isomorphism

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 68.5; the frame is a basis

definition.condition: the root frame is a simple system of type Gamma

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 68.5; a fixed finite Dynkin type on S

definition.condition: an isometric embedding i : L -> J together with a root basis on L, equivalently its image in J
definition.notes: Foundations Def 68.5: a locus in Ar(Lat_Z). A named ADE construction is a chosen point here followed by the forgetful functors that discard, successively, the ambient embedding, the root basis, and the type certificate (Construction 68.6).

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 68.5; ordinary classifier categories, not a bespoke mechanism
Sage Algebras = associative unital; MagmaticAlgebras is the Wikipedia algebra
Modules over a field; not a separate primitive

definition.condition: presentable symmetric monoidal, tensor preserving colimits separately in each variable, and O such that free O-algebras exist
definition.notes: add-II Def 11.1. These hypotheses are part of every theorem below and cannot be replaced by a bare assertion (Prop 12.4).

add-II Def 11.1: the O-algebra objects of V. Rmk 11.3: under standard hypotheses U_O is monadic, which identifies Alg_O(V) with algebras for the monad U_O Free_O -- it does NOT identify all O-algebras with the essential image of the free functor.

entities.provenance: add-II Def 11.1; all algebra objects, not the free ones

definition.generators: objects 0,1; f : 0 -> 1; u : 1 -> 0; eta : id_0 => uf; epsilon : fu => id_1
definition.notes: add-II Def 1.1. A morphism of (infinity,2)-categories out of it selects an adjunction.
definition.relations: the triangle coherences together with all higher coherences forced by the universal property

entities.provenance: add-II Def 1.1; the single mathematical home for units, counits and triangle coherences. Deliberately carries no dot_vertex: the parent graph presents categories and the structural functors between them, and this is an INDEXING SHAPE in (infinity,2)-Cat, used as the domain of the functor category Adj(K) = Fun(WalkingAdjunction, K). It has no structural functor to or from any category in the graph, and inventing one to make it a vertex would encode a relation that does not exist.

definition.notes: add-II Def 1.2. An object is C, D, F : C -> D, U : D -> C, a unit, a counit and coherent triangle data. Evaluation on the generating 1-cells gives ev_F, ev_U into Arr(K); evaluation on the generating 2-cells gives the unit and counit as points of the appropriate mapping categories. So the unit and counit are NOT separate primitives (Def 1.2), and the chosen adjunction structures on a fixed pair (F,U) are the homotopy fibre of (ev_F, ev_U) (Def 1.3). RAdj(F) and LAdj(U) are the fibres of Def 1.4, empty or contractible by Thm 1.5, which is why existence is property-like while a chosen lift is data.

entities.provenance: add-II Def 1.2; the target of every named chosen adjunction lift required by Conv 1.7

definition.formula: Pres(F) := C^~ x^h_{D^~, F^~, ev_0} Arr(D^~), with p_F : Pres(F) -> D^~ evaluating at the target
definition.notes: add-II Def 3.1. An object is (X, Y, phi) with phi : F(X) ~-> Y. Prop 3.3: X |-> (X, F(X), id) is an EQUIVALENCE from C^~, so this is not a new total object -- its value is the target projection p_F, whose fibres Pres_F(Y) are the chosen-presentation spaces (Prop 3.2). Membership in the essential image is the propositional truncation of Pres_F(Y). Conv 13.1: C, EssIm(F) and the fibres of p_F are never identified without the relevant comparison theorem.

entities.provenance: add-II Def 3.1; the standard comma construction on cores, named only because its projection is used

definition.condition: an integral inclusion L <= L' <= L^# with L'/L finite; even if the restricted form on L' is even
definition.notes: Foundations Def 27.1. Explicitly a locus in Ar(Lat_Z), or a category over L whose morphisms are isometries compatible with the inclusions. Construction 27.3 builds L_H from a subgroup H of A_L; Thm 27.4 is the classical correspondence with isotropic subgroups and is a THEOREM, not part of the definition.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 27.1; a locus in the arrow category of lattices

definition.condition: H <= A with q|_H = 0 (for a bilinear discriminant form, b|_{HxH} = 0)
definition.notes: Foundations Def 27.2. Subgroup-hood is a carried inclusion morphism (a subobject), not a bare-object property; the isotropy condition cuts out the locus. Gluing (Constr 27.6) forms the graph of an anti-isometry, isotropic for q_1 (+) q_2, and the corresponding overlattice -- a theorem built from Thm 27.4, again not definitional.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 27.2; the isotropy locus among discriminant-form subobjects

definition.notes: Foundations Def 35.1: a moduli problem on a site is a pseudofunctor C^op -> Grpd, invariantly a stack of spaces after imposing descent -- i.e. a prestack, and a stack once it satisfies descent. An object of M(T) is a family over T and pullback is base change. Named for its use; the underlying category is the prestack one.

entities.provenance: Foundations Def 35.1; a prestack, viewed as a moduli problem

definition.condition: M ~ h_M is representable; the identity point of h_M(M) is the universal family
definition.notes: Foundations Def 35.2. Fine representability is a property of a moduli problem, not a separate kind of object.
